div.h2: Line Tracking Inside Multi-Line Block Comment via advance()

div.p: Key Invariant: advance() is the single source of truth for position — called unconditionally inside _scan_block_comment(), so newlines inside comments are never missed. After */ closes, _begin_token() snapshots the correct position for the next real token (x on line 2).

g.text: ★ Newline Transition Detail — advance() called for '\n' at index 7
Call | idx | char | line_before | col_before | line_after | col_after | note
── peek()=='*' branch: consume '*' then peek()=='/' → consume '/' → RETURN ──────────

div.p: Token(IDENTIFIER, 'x', line=2, column=9) ✓ reflects start of 'x', not start of comment
current = 8 line = 2 ← incremented column = 1 ← reset to 1
_scan_block_comment() calls advance() for every character — including \n. No special newline-handling code is needed in the comment scanner itself.
